back_translation: 'These are the ordinances that you shall set before them:'
'If you buy a Hebrew servant, he shall serve you for six years. In the seventh year, he shall go free without paying anything.'
'If he came alone, he shall leave alone. If he had a wife, his wife shall leave with him.'
'If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the woman and her children shall belong to her master, and only he shall leave alone.'
'But if the servant clearly declares, I love my master, my wife, and my children — I do not want to go free,'
'then his master shall bring him before the judges. He shall bring him to the door or the doorpost, and his master shall pierce his ear with an awl, and he shall serve him forever.'
'If a man sells his daughter as a maidservant, she shall not go free as the menservants do.'

'If she is displeasing in the eyes of her master who has designated her for himself, he shall let her be redeemed. He has no authority to sell her to a foreign people, since he has acted unfaithfully toward her.'
'If he designates her for his son, he shall deal with her according to the rights of daughters.'
'If he takes another wife, he shall not reduce her food, her clothing, or her marital rights.'
'If he does not do these three things for her, she shall go free, without payment of money.'
'Whoever strikes a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death.'
'But if he did not lie in wait, and God allowed it to come into his hand, then I will appoint for you a place to which he may flee.'
'But if a man schemes against his neighbor to kill him willfully, you shall take him from my altar to be put to death.'
'Whoever strikes his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.'
'Whoever kidnaps a man and sells him, or is found with him in his possession, shall surely be put to death.'
'Whoever curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.'
'If men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or with his fist, and he does not die but is confined to bed,'
'if he gets up and walks around outside with his staff, the one who struck him shall be acquitted. He shall only pay for his loss of time and ensure that he is fully healed.'
'If a man strikes his manservant or maidservant with a rod, and the servant dies under his hand, he shall surely be punished.'
'However, if the servant survives a day or two, he shall not be punished, for the servant is his property.'
'If men fight and strike a pregnant woman, and her children come out, but there is no further injury, he shall surely be fined as the woman's husband demands and as the judges allow.'
'But if there is injury, then you shall give life for life,'
'eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,'
'burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.'
'If a man strikes the eye of his manservant or the eye of his maidservant and destroys it, he shall let him go free as compensation for his eye.'
'And if he knocks out the tooth of his manservant or maidservant, he shall let him go free as compensation for his tooth.'
'If an ox gores a man or woman so that they die, the ox shall surely be stoned and its meat shall not be eaten, but the owner of the ox shall be acquitted.'

'But if the ox was prone to gore from a previous time, and its owner had been warned but did not restrain it, and it killed a man or woman, the ox shall be stoned, and its owner also shall be put to death.'
'If a ransom is imposed upon him, he shall give the redemption of his life according to all that is imposed upon him.'
'Whether it gores a son or a daughter, according to this rule it shall be done to him.'
'If the ox gores a manservant or maidservant, thirty shekels of silver shall be given to the master, and the ox shall be stoned.'
'If a man opens a pit, or if a man digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,'
'the owner of the pit shall make compensation; he shall return money to its owner, and the dead animal shall be his.'
'If a man's ox strikes his neighbor's ox and it dies, they shall sell the live ox and divide its money, and they shall also divide the dead one.'
'Or if it was known that the ox was prone to gore from a previous time, and its owner did not restrain it, he shall surely repay an ox for the ox, and the dead one shall be his.'
'If a man steals an ox or a sheep, and slaughters it or sells it, he shall pay five oxen for the ox, and four sheep for the sheep.'
